summary: A promotional blog post for a Udacity Talks Q&A session featuring Sebastian Thrun discussing the Self-Driving Car Engineer Nanodegree. The text highlights the rapid historical progress of autonomous vehicles since the DARPA Grand Challenges and emphasizes Thrun's belief that realizing the future of self-driving cars requires a global influx of diverse engineering talent.

principles.statement: Making autonomous vehicles a reality requires a global influx of diverse talent.
principles.rationale: The field needs the best inventors, dreamers, and mavericks from all over the world to drive the technology forward.
principles.source_quote: To make autonomous vehicles a reality we will need the best inventors, dreamers and mavericks to come into this field from wherever they might be in the world.

signature_quotes.text: To make autonomous vehicles a reality we will need the best inventors, dreamers and mavericks to come into this field from wherever they might be in the world.
signature_quotes.context: Discussing the need for a new generation of engineers to advance self-driving car technology.

signature_quotes.text: In 2004, DARPA held the first Grand Challenge for an autonomous car to drive 142 miles through the Mojave desert in under 10 hours. Fifteen cars participated, none finished. In 2005, the Challenge was repeated and 23 cars entered. Four finished under 10 hours and our car “Stanley” won in 6 hours and 53 minutes—11 minutes ahead of the next car.
signature_quotes.context: Recounting the rapid early progress of autonomous vehicles from the first DARPA Grand Challenge.
